translated: Jesus went around to all the towns and villages, teaching in their synagogues and proclaiming the good news of the kingdom, healing every disease and sickness.

ref: The chief priests and the whole council wanted to put Jesus to death. So they tried to find some people who would tell lies about him in court.
text: Koosa dam kɔkɔɔrsi ta awa sɛria nidiile ta maga funɛ nyɛn dooni a nyini di ba na nandigi o wa ba suwachak a wa ŋɔ ba di o na Yesu ka o tum tum tagna, chen ni ba zi na nyinbaanu a kpu un.
translated: The reason we are happy today is because of the great love that God showed to us by sending his only Son, Jesus, who came and died for us so that we might have eternal life.

ref: So when you go to a town or a village, find someone able and willing to have you as their guest and stay with them until you leave.
text: “Ma wa la tel tɔha, ma zu a nyini naa la zi la ma a du. Ma nyɛn doon hali a wuzu naa ma wa li tɔhɔn ti ni.
translated: We did not know the road, we just followed the path until we reached where we were going. We walked until we arrived at the place we did not know.

ref: The disciples answered, “Some people say you are John the Baptist or maybe Elijah or Jeremiah or some other prophet.”
text: Ba fun ŋɔ, “Bɔnɛ yaa yir hin Jɔn naa la chaksi nara wa ni ni chen. Bɔnɛ ka yir hin Ɛlaija. Bɔnɛ ka ŋɔ di Jɛrɛmaya koo Koosa witinsi tima digi bur wa.”
translated: Then he said, “A man named John came to you and you did not believe him. A man named Elijah. This man is either Jeremiah or one of the prophets.”

ref: When the disciples heard this, they were greatly surprised and asked, “How can anyone ever be saved?”
text: O sɛm ta fun ya o gantɛl tiŋsi ta alaazibu kinkan a kaali a maga. Ba fun ŋɔ un, “O chi nan, anti la zi koti a zu Koosa koor ti?”
translated: He said to his friend that he would go to the farm tomorrow morning. His friend replied, “Is it true, or are you just teasing me?”

ref: Jesus answered, “I have just one question to ask you. If you answer it, I will tell you where I got the right to do these things.
text: Yesu fun ŋɔ ba, “N gbanko kpak pɛɛsiu a zi pɛɛsi ma. N pɛɛsi ma ka ma koti a ŋɔ min o mun, n gbanko zi biksi ma naa nyɛ kin kpak ti muu tum kin maa maga chenu.
translated: Jesus said to them, “I am the bread of life. Whoever comes to me will never go hungry, and whoever believes in me will never be thirsty.

ref: Everyone ate all they wanted, and the leftovers filled seven large baskets.
text: Ba fun se puŋ a ka. Ba funɛ se peti chen, Yesu gantɛl tiŋsi ta fun pa ba kidikaalɛ a su chenchisa nɔpɛ.
translated: They said that he was a good person. They said that when Jesus comes back to earth, he will reward them for their kindness to strangers.

ref: “We played the flute, but you would not dance! We sang a funeral song, but you would not mourn!”
text: ‘Ya funɛ mo gurou a du ma, amaa ma ka wa fun gɔ. Ya funɛ lɔ zɛɛm boolau, amaa ma tusa ka wa fun chɔk.’
translated: You said you would go to the farm, but you did not go. You said you would fetch water, but again you did not go.

ref: Suddenly a Canaanite woman from there came out shouting, “Lord and Son of David, have pity on me! My daughter is full of demons.”
text: Ba fun lul haan digi dooni. O fun wa sumi Yesu, “Mu kunyan koor Devid bi luli, kpak min siki. Alizin tagin kanɛ mu nihabichɛl nyuhu ni a vɛ weri.”
translated: They went and entered the town. Jesus said to them, “Let us go to the village of David, for it is near. Prepare yourselves so that we may not be ashamed when we arrive.”

ref: But Jesus ordered them not to tell anyone what had happened. Then he said, “Give her something to eat.”
text: Yesu fun ŋɔ di ba ya diwe a nyɛ be ti ni o di, a fun ŋɔ ba kpok ni, “Ma ta nyɛ nandigi zim kinkin naa yɛɛ.”
translated: Jesus said to them, “Bring some of the fish you have just caught.”

ref: At noon the sky turned dark and stayed that way until three o'clock.
text: Nha funɛ wa tel sal baan nyuha, doon fun wa biri a vusi hali saha naa niparɛ yaa par a si chen.
translated: If you want to go to the market tomorrow, you should wake up early so that you can go and return quickly.

ref: Even the hairs on your head are counted.
text: Ma wa wara, hali Koosa funɛ gur ma nyuksau a li ma nyuuni ni.
translated: We are going to the farm, but Koosa did not go with us because he is sick.
